revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''都出 比呂志'''（つで ひろし、[[1942年]][[1月1日]] - ）は、日本の[[考古学者]]。[[大阪大学]][[名誉教授]]。 ==来歴== 1964年...'
revision.text:
'''都出 比呂志'''（つで ひろし、[[1942年]][[1月1日]] - ）は、日本の[[考古学者]]。[[大阪大学]][[名誉教授]]。
==来歴== 1964年、[[京都大学]]文学部を卒業、1968年、京都大学大学院文学研究科博士課程を中退し、京都大学文学部助手に就任。1977年から[[滋賀大学]]教育学部助教授、1979年から[[大阪大学]]文学部助教授、1988年から同教授。2003年に退職し、名誉教授。
==受賞歴== *1989年、[[浜田青陵]]賞 *1997年、[[雄山閣]]考古学特別賞 *2004年、[[八日市市]]教育功労賞
==著書== ===単著=== *『竪穴式石室の地域性の研究』 大阪大学文学部国史研究室、1986年 *『日本農耕社会の成立過程』 岩波書店、1989年 *『王陵の考古学』 岩波書店、2000年 *『前方後円墳と社会』 塙書房、2005年 ===編著=== *『古墳時代の王と民衆』 講談社、1989年 *『邪馬台国の時代』 木耳社、1990年 *『日本古代の葬制と社会関係の基礎的研究』 大阪大学文学部、1995年 *『古代史の論点4 権力と国家と戦争』 小学館、1998年 *『古代国家はこうして生まれた』 角川書店、1998年 *『古代史の論点2 女と男、家と村』 小学館、2000年 *『古代史の論点1 環境と食料生産』 小学館、2000年
{{DEFAULTSORT:つて ひろし}} [[Category:日本の考古学者]] [[Category:1942年生]]

revision.comment: 記述の一部を加筆・修正
revision.text:
'''都出 比呂志'''（つで ひろし、[[1942年]][[1月1日]] - ）は、日本の[[考古学者]]。[[大阪大学]][[名誉教授]]。[[文学博士]]（大阪大学、1989年）。
==略歴== *1964年3月 [[京都大学]][[文学部]]卒業 *1968年7月 京都大学[[大学院]]文学研究科考古学専攻[[博士課程]]中退 *1968年8月 京都大学文学部[[助手]] *1977年4月 [[滋賀大学]][[教育学部]][[助教授]] *1979年4月 大阪大学文学部助教授 *1988年4月 大阪大学文学部[[教授]] *1999年4月 大阪大学大学院文学研究科教授 *2003年 退職 名誉教授
==受賞歴== *1989年、[[浜田青陵]]賞 *1997年、[[雄山閣]]考古学特別賞 *2004年、[[八日市市]]教育功労賞
==著書== ===単著=== *『竪穴式石室の地域性の研究』 大阪大学文学部国史研究室、1986年 *『日本農耕社会の成立過程』 岩波書店、1989年 *『王陵の考古学』 岩波書店、2000年 *『前方後円墳と社会』 塙書房、2005年 ===編著=== *『古墳時代の王と民衆』 講談社、1989年 *『邪馬台国の時代』 木耳社、1990年 *『日本古代の葬制と社会関係の基礎的研究』 大阪大学文学部、1995年 *『古代史の論点4 権力と国家と戦争』 小学館、1998年 *『古代国家はこうして生まれた』 角川書店、1998年 *『古代史の論点2 女と男、家と村』 小学館、2000年 *『古代史の論点1 環境と食料生産』 小学館、2000年

'''都出 比呂志'''（つで ひろし、[[1942年]][[1月1日]] - ）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]]出身の日本の[[考古学者]]。[[大阪大学]]名誉教授。[[文学博士]]（大阪大学、1989年）。初期国家論をもとに[[前方後円墳体制]]論を提唱した。但し、前方後円墳から政治体制が導き出されるのか、墓作りと権力の関係・宗教との関係・文化との関係など、歴史学者からは問題提議が為されている。都出説は依然として仮説の幾を出ていない。

'''都出 比呂志'''（つで ひろし、[[1942年]][[1月1日]] - ）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]]出身の日本の[[考古学者]]。[[大阪大学]]名誉教授。[[文学博士]]（大阪大学、1989年）。初期国家論をもとに[[前方後円墳体制]]論を提唱した。但し、前方後円墳から政治体制が導き出されるのか、墓作りと権力の関係・宗教との関係・文化との関係など歴史学者からは問題提議が為されており、多くの古代史学者は支持していない。都出説は依然として仮説の幾を出ていないと言える。支配とは何か、支配を維持する体制、支配を受け入れる仕組みなど、基本的な概念規定に問題があると言える。古代史学者から評価されない所以であろう。
==略歴== * 1964年3月 [[京都大学]][[文学部]]卒業 * 1968年7月 京都大学[[大学院]]文学研究科考古学専攻[[博士課程]]中退 * 1968年8月 京都大学文学部[[助手 (教育)|助手]] * 1977年4月 [[滋賀大学]][[教育学部]][[助教授]] * 1979年4月 大阪大学文学部助教授 * 1988年4月 大阪大学文学部[[教授]] * 1999年4月 大阪大学大学院文学研究科教授 * 2003年 退職。名誉教授
== 受賞歴 == * 1989年、[[浜田青陵]]賞 * 1997年、[[雄山閣]]考古学特別賞 * 2004年、[[八日市市]]教育功労賞
== 著書 == === 単著 === * 『竪穴式石室の地域性の研究』 大阪大学文学部国史研究室、1986年 * 『日本農耕社会の成立過程』 岩波書店、1989年 * 『王陵の考古学』 岩波書店、2000年 * 『前方後円墳と社会』 塙書房、2005年 * 『古代国家はいつ成立したか』 岩波新書、岩波書店、2011年 === 編著 === * 『古墳時代の王と民衆』 講談社、1989年 * 『邪馬台国の時代』 木耳社、1990年 * 『日本古代の葬制と社会関係の基礎的研究』 大阪大学文学部、1995年 * 『古代史の論点4 権力と国家と戦争』 小学館、1998年 * 『古代国家はこうして生まれた』 角川書店、1998年 * 『古代史の論点2 女と男、家と村』 小学館、2000年 * 『古代史の論点1 環境と食料生産』 小学館、2000年 == 他 == * 「[[乙訓の文化遺産を守る会]]」会長([[中山修一]]1966年設立）

=== 編著 === * 『古墳時代の王と民衆』講談社、1989年 * 『邪馬台国の時代』木耳社、1990年 * 『日本古代の葬制と社会関係の基礎的研究』大阪大学文学部、1995年 * 『古代史の論点4 権力と国家と戦争』小学館、1998年 * 『古代国家はこうして生まれた』角川書店、1998年 * 『古代史の論点2 女と男、家と村』小学館、2000年 * 『古代史の論点1 環境と食料生産』小学館、2000年
== 他 == * 「[[乙訓の文化遺産を守る会]]」前会長（[[中山修一]]1966年設立）

'''都出 比呂志'''（つで ひろし、[[1942年]][[1月1日]] - ）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]]出身の日本の[[考古学]]者。[[大阪大学]]名誉教授。[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]（[[大阪大学]]・[[博士#博士学位の取得方法|論文博士]]・1989年）。初期国家論を基に[[前方後円墳体制]]論を提唱した。
== 略歴 == * 1964年3月 [[京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|京都大学文学部]]卒業 * 1968年7月 京都大学大学院文学研究科考古学専攻博士課程中退 * 1968年8月 京都大学文学部[[助手 (教育)|助手]] * 1977年4月 [[滋賀大学]][[教育学部]][[助教授]] * 1979年4月 [[大阪大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|大阪大学文学部]]助教授 * 1988年4月 大阪大学文学部[[教授]] * 1999年4月 大阪大学大学院文学研究科教授 * 2003年 退職。[[名誉教授]]
== 受賞歴 == * 1989年、[[浜田青陵]]賞 * 1997年、[[雄山閣]]考古学特別賞（『[[雪野山古墳]]の研究』） * 2004年、[[八日市市]]教育功労賞 *2018年、第19回和島誠一賞
== 著書 == === 単著 === * 『竪穴式石室の地域性の研究』大阪大学文学部国史研究室、1986年 * 『日本農耕社会の成立過程』岩波書店、1989年 * 『王陵の考古学』岩波書店、2000年 * 『前方後円墳と社会』塙書房、2005年 * 『古代国家はいつ成立したか』[[岩波新書]]、岩波書店、2011年 * 『古墳時代に魅せられて』[[大阪大学出版会]]〈阪大リーブル 65〉、2018年
=== 編著 === * 『古墳時代の王と民衆』講談社、1989年 * 『邪馬台国の時代』木耳社、1990年 * 『日本古代の葬制と社会関係の基礎的研究』[[大阪大学文学部]]、1995年 * 『古代史の論点4 権力と国家と戦争』小学館、1998年 * 『古代国家はこうして生まれた』角川書店、1998年 * 『古代史の論点2 女と男、家と村』小学館、2000年 * 『古代史の論点1 環境と食料生産』小学館、2000年
== 他 == * 「[[乙訓の文化遺産を守る会]]」前会長（[[中山修一]]1966年設立）

revision.text:
'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: Appointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: Appointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Tumulus]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].
{{Normdaten}}
[[Category:Japanese archaeologists]] [[Category:Osaka University faculty]] [[Category:Shiga University faculty]] [[Category:Kyoto University alumni]] [[Category:People from Osaka City]] [[Category:1942 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[ja:都出比呂志]]

'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: Appointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: Appointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Tumulus]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].
{{Normdaten}}

[[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.

* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Tumulus]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].

revision.text:
'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: Appointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: Appointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Tumulus]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].
{{Normdaten}}

'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: Appointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: Appointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Tumulus]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].
{{Normdaten}}

Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.

[[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.

'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: Appointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: Appointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.

* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Tumulus]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.

'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: Appointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: Appointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Tumulus]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].
{{Normdaten}}

'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: Appointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: Appointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Tumulus]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].
{{Normdaten}}

revision.text:
'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: Appointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: Appointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Tumulus]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].
{{Normdaten}}

'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: Aqqointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: Aqqointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Tumulus]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].
{{Normdaten}}

'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: A11ointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: A11ointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Kofun]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].
{{Normdaten}}

'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: A11ointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: A11ointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Kofun]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].
{{Normdaten}}

revision.comment: 27 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:都出比呂志 up to rev 92768035
revision.text:
'''都出 比呂志'''（つで ひろし、[[1942年]][[1月1日]] - ）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]]出身の[[日本]]の[[考古学]]者。[[大阪大学]]名誉教授。[[博士（文学）|文学博士]]（[[大阪大学]]・[[博士#博士学位の取得方法|論文博士]]・1989年）。初期国家論を基に[[前方後円墳体制]]論を提唱した。
== 略歴 == * 1964年3月 [[京都大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|京都大学文学部]]卒業 * 1968年7月 京都大学大学院文学研究科考古学専攻博士課程中退 * 1968年8月 京都大学文学部[[助手 (教育)|助手]] * 1977年4月 [[滋賀大学]][[教育学部]][[助教授]] * 1979年4月 [[大阪大学大学院文学研究科・文学部|大阪大学文学部]]助教授 * 1988年4月 大阪大学文学部[[教授]] * 1999年4月 大阪大学大学院文学研究科教授 * 2003年 退職。[[名誉教授]]
== 受賞歴 == * 1989年、[[浜田青陵]]賞 * 1997年、[[雄山閣]]考古学特別賞（『[[雪野山古墳]]の研究』） * 2004年、[[八日市市]]教育功労賞 *2018年、第19回和島誠一賞
== 著書 == === 単著 === * 『竪穴式石室の地域性の研究』大阪大学文学部国史研究室、1986年 * 『日本農耕社会の成立過程』岩波書店、1989年 * 『王陵の考古学』岩波書店、2000年 * 『前方後円墳と社会』塙書房、2005年 * 『古代国家はいつ成立したか』[[岩波新書]]、岩波書店、2011年 * 『古墳時代に魅せられて』[[大阪大学出版会]]〈阪大リーブル 65〉、2018年
=== 編著 === * 『古墳時代の王と民衆』講談社、1989年 * 『邪馬台国の時代』木耳社、1990年 * 『日本古代の葬制と社会関係の基礎的研究』[[大阪大学文学部]]、1995年 * 『古代史の論点4 権力と国家と戦争』小学館、1998年 * 『古代国家はこうして生まれた』角川書店、1998年 * 『古代史の論点2 女と男、家と村』小学館、2000年 * 『古代史の論点1 環境と食料生産』小学館、2000年
== 他 == * 「[[乙訓の文化遺産を守る会]]」前会長（[[中山修一]]1966年設立）
{{Normdaten}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:つて ひろし}} [[Category:日本の考古学者]] [[Category:大阪大学の教員]] [[Category:滋賀大学の教員]] [[Category:京都大学出身の人物]] [[Category:大阪市出身の人物]] [[Category:1942年生]] [[Category:存命人物]]

{{Normdaten}}

'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[January 1]], [[1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: A11ointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: A11ointed associate professor at [[Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Kofun]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.

revision.text:
'''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[:en:January 1|January 1]], [[:en:1942|1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[:en:Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[:en:Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University|Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: A11ointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: A11ointed associate professor at [[:en:Shiga University|Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Kofun]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|qq=}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].

=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:都出比呂志]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1942|1|1}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}|native_name=都出比呂志|given_name={{ill|Hiroshi|WD=Q30098041}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11643486]] Wikidata name: Hiroshi Tsude [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born [[:en:January 1|January 1]], [[:en:1942|1942]]) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. He is an emeritus professor at [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[:en:Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[:en:Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University|Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: A11ointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: A11ointed associate professor at [[:en:Shiga University|Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Kofun]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==

He is an emeritus professor at [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.

[[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.

He is an emeritus professor at [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[:en:Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[:en:Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University|Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: A11ointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: A11ointed associate professor at [[:en:Shiga University|Shiga University]] Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Kofun]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].

* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Kofun]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', [[:en:Kodansha|Kodansha]], 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', [[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]], 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.

* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Kofun]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:都出比呂志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:都出比呂志]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:都出比呂志]] {{Infobox person|birth_date={{birth date|1942|1|1}}|birth_place={{ill|Osaka|WD=Q35765}}|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|occupation={{ill|archaeologist|WD=Q3621491}}|native_name=都出比呂志|given_name={{ill|Hiroshi|WD=Q30098041}}}} '''Tsude Hiroshi''' (都出 比呂志, born {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}, {{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942}}) is a Japanese archaeologist from [[:en:Osaka City|Osaka City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. He is an emeritus professor at [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] and holds a Doctorate in Literature (awarded by Osaka University in 1989). Tsude is known for his theory on the [[Keyhole-shaped tumulus system]] based on early state formation studies.
== Biography ==
* March 1964: Graduated from the [[:en:Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University|Faculty of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * July 1968: Withdrew from the doctoral program in archaeology at the [[:en:Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University|Graduate School of Letters, Kyoto University]]. * August 1968: A11ointed assistant at Kyoto University Faculty of Letters. * April 1977: A11ointed associate professor at {{ill|Shiga University|en|Shiga University|WD=Q1072796|lt=Shiga University}} Faculty of Education. * April 1979: Associate professor at [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] Faculty of Letters. * April 1988: Promoted to professor at Osaka University Faculty of Letters. * April 1999: Professor at [[Graduate School of Letters, Osaka University]]. * 2003: Retired and granted emeritus professor status.
== Awards ==
* 1989: [[Hamada Seiryo Prize]]. * 1997: Special Archaeology Award from [[Yuzankaku Publishing]] (for ''Research on the [[Yukinoyama Kofun]]''). * 2004: Education Merit Award from [[Yokaichi City]]. * 2018: 19th [[Wajima Seiichi Prize]].
== Publications ==
=== Single-authored books === * ''Research on the Regional Characteristics of Pit-style Stone Chambers'', Osaka University Historical Research Office, 1986. * ''Formation Process of Japan’s Agricultural Society'', {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|en|Iwanami Shoten|WD=Q1331427|lt=Iwanami Shoten}}, 1989. * ''Archaeology of Royal Tombs'', Iwanami Shoten, 2000. * ''Keyhole-shaped Tumuli and Society'', [[Hanawa Shobo]], 2005. * ''When Was the Ancient State Established?'' [[Iwanami Shinsho]], Iwanami Shoten, 2011. * ''Fascinated by the Kofun Period'', [[Osaka University Press]], 2018.
=== Edited volumes === * ''Kings and Commoners of the Kofun Period'', {{ill|Kodansha|en|Kodansha|WD=Q726081|lt=Kodansha}}, 1989. * ''The Era of Yamatai-koku'', [[Mokuyusha]], 1990. * ''Basic Research on Funeral Systems and Social Relations in Ancient Japan'', Osaka University Faculty of Letters, 1995. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 4: Power, States, and War'', [[Shogakukan]], 1998. * ''How the Ancient State Was Born'', {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|WD=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten}}, 1998. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 2: Women and Men, Houses and Villages'', Shogakukan, 2000. * ''Key Issues in Ancient History 1: Environment and Food Production'', Shogakukan, 2000.
== Other Activities == * Former chairman of the ''{{ill|Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|ja|乙訓の文化遺産を守る会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|1=Association for Protecting the Cultural Heritage of Otsukuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369956}}'', founded in 1966 by [[Nakayama Shuichi]].
{{translated page|ja|都出比呂志|version=92768035|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:都出比呂志]]
